commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a detailed `/status plugins` diagnostic for configured memory embedding providers that are not registered by the active runtime and shares the registered-provider-id collection used by gateway startup.

PR surface: Source +79, Tests +170. Total +249 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main’s `/status plugins` path has the startup warning helper but no detailed status field or rendering for unregistered configured memory embedding providers; the PR body also includes after-fix live output from the real handler path.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is normal maintainer review plus exact-head CI and merge gating.

target.title: fix(status): surface unregistered memory embedding providers
target.body:
Related: #97479, #97878

## What Problem This Solves

When a memory embedding provider is configured (`agents.*.memorySearch.provider` or its fallback) but no loaded plugin registers a provider that can serve it, semantic memory recall silently falls back to keyword/FTS-only search. Today the only signal is a one-time gateway startup log line — the `/status plugins` view shows nothing, so an operator asking "is my configured memory provider actually available?" gets no answer from status.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds an observer-only line to the detailed `/status plugins` view that surfaces configured memory embedding providers no loaded plugin registers, reusing the exact configured-vs-registered comparison the gateway already runs at startup (`collectUnregisteredConfiguredMemoryEmbeddingProviders`). To keep the status check from drifting from gateway-boot semantics, the boot path's three-source "registered provider ids" assembly is extracted into a small shared helper (`collectRegisteredEmbeddingProviderIds`) used by both boot and status (behavior-preserving extraction). It is detailed-status only — the compact line is untouched — and it needs the live runtime registry, so it renders in-gateway and degrades to no line when the registry is unavailable (for example a fresh CLI process), when config or memory search is absent, or when the resolver returns nothing. Nothing is reclassified as an error. There is no repair, auto-enable, config mutation, or change to startup, provider, doctor, or channel behavior.

## User Impact

Operators running `/status plugins` (detailed) now see a clear, non-error line — `Configured memory provider not registered: N (<id> (memorySearch.<source>), …)` — when a configured memory embedding provider is not served by any loaded plugin, so they can install or enable the owning plugin instead of silently getting degraded keyword-only memory recall. Nothing changes for users whose providers are registered, and the compact `/status` line is unchanged.

## Evidence

Builds on #97479 (runtime-loaded vs installed) and #97878 (should-run plugin drift).

HEAD: `0bcf7df47bf8e4eaa5792969931cb628ba5e48b6`

**Real behavior proof** — the real `/status plugins` handler `buildStatusPluginsReply` exercised on the production path via `node --import tsx`, isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, no mocks. Config sets `agents.defaults.memorySearch.provider = "nonexistent-embed-proof"` (a provider no plugin serves):

[1] active runtime registry, provider not registered → the line appears; compact line unchanged:
```
🔌 Plugins: OK
Loaded: 0
Disabled: 60
Installed (not active): 67 (alibaba, amazon-bedrock, amazon-bedrock-mantle, anthropic, anthropic-vertex, arcee, azure-speech, byteplus, +59 more)
Configured to run but not loaded: 8 (acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice)
Configured memory provider not registered: 1 (nonexistent-embed-proof (memorySearch.provider))
Full inventory: /plugins list
```
[2] the same provider then registered (`registerEmbeddingProvider`) → the line is absent (no false positive once the owning plugin registers).
[3] no active runtime registry (fresh CLI-like process) → the line is absent (the null-registry guard prevents a CLI false positive); the rest of the output is unchanged.

**Targeted tests and checks (local):**
- vitest: status formatter (line appears with entries / absent when empty or field absent / compact `🔌 Plugins: OK` unchanged); collector (entries attached and checked against the registered-id set / registered → none / **null registry → no field** / no config → none); embedding-provider helper boot-equivalence (three-source union + empty-registry = global ids only). All pass.
- `tsgo:core` (prod) and `tsgo:core:test` exit 0; `pnpm build` exits 0 with no `[INEFFECTIVE_DYNAMIC_IMPORT]`; `pnpm check:import-cycles` reports 0 cycles; `oxfmt --check` clean.
- AI review: `codex review` clean; GPT-5.5 review clean.

**Known limitations:** the diagnostic needs the live in-gateway runtime registry to know which providers a loaded plugin serves, so it renders on the in-gateway `/status plugins` surface (consistent with the existing runtime-loaded and should-run status diagnostics); a bare CLI `openclaw status` process has no active registry and degrades to no line.
</content>
target.author: masatohoshino
